summary: Mechanical review complete. The old global PoolManager singleton and verifier-time synchronous pool mutation API are intentionally absent in the v2 port. The current design removes originals during canonical-state async commitment processing, records async metadata, locks senders, and uses block-verifier origin/nonce-lock checks rather than mutating the txpool during verification. No confirmed FSM/runtime gap remains, but this is a compatibility/design-change row worth confirming if verifier-time txpool mutation was ever a required interface.
notes: Reviewed against latest code. Treat this as legacy API/architecture difference: canonical-state cleanup plus nonce-lock verification replaces the old global singleton; remaining concerns are support/design-confirmation and targeted regression tests, not known missing runtime behavior.
